sections.paragraph:
θηλυκός, ή, όν (Aristot. et al.; PLille 10, 5 [III b.c.]; POxy 1458, 11; PGM 4, 2518; Num 5:3; Dt 4:16; TestSol; Philo, Deus Imm. 141) pert. to being female, female ἵνα ἀδελφὸς ἰδὼν ἀδελφὴν οὐδὲν φρονῇ περὶ αὐτῆς θηλυκόν that a male member when he sees a female member should not think of her as a female 2 Cl 12:5.—DELG s.v. θηλή.

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PLille PLille = Papyrus grecs de Lille—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 455
